name: n8n Ops
description: Ops-focused n8n tools for OpenClaw agents: list, inspect, trigger, validate, full workflow + execution lifecycle (create, save, archive, delete, cancel, retry single + batch), tag management, security audit, plus composed scanners for cross-cutting workflow questions. Auto-backup + confirm gates on destructive writes.

baseUrl.description: Base URL of the n8n instance, e.g. http://n8n.example.internal:5678

apiKey.description: n8n public API key (X-N8N-API-KEY). Leave blank and set N8N_API_KEY in the environment instead.

apiKeyEnv.description: Environment variable to read the API key from when apiKey is blank.

enableEdit.description: Enable all workflow + execution lifecycle write tools (create, activate, deactivate, save, archive, unarchive, delete-workflow, cancel-execution, retry-execution, retry-executions, delete-execution, delete-executions, pin-node-data, unpin-node-data, create-tag, delete-tag, set-workflow-tags). Off by default. Destructive tools (save, delete-workflow, delete-execution, delete-executions, delete-tag, retry-executions) are confirm-gated; save and delete-workflow snapshot to backupDir first. Does NOT enable credential writes — those need enableCredentialsWrite as a second gate.

enableCredentialsWrite.description: Second gate (on top of enableEdit) for credential write tools (create-credential, delete-credential). Off by default. The only path in this plugin where agent input contains plaintext secrets (create) and where deletion cascades to break every workflow referencing the credential (delete). Forces explicit opt-in: an agent that has been overprovisioned with enableEdit cannot inject or destroy credentials without a separate, deliberate config change. Both flags must be true for credential writes to register; the read tools (list-credentials, get-credential-schema, find-workflows-using-credential) and the disabled-node scanner are always available.

maxExecutionLogBytes.description: Max bytes of execution log returned inline. Larger logs are truncated with a tail hint.

requestTimeoutMs.description: HTTP timeout for n8n API calls.

backupDir.description: Directory for pre-save workflow snapshots. Defaults to ~/.n8n-backups.

baseUrl.label: Base URL
baseUrl.help: Your n8n instance, e.g. http://host:5678. Container-direct IPs work fine if routable.

apiKey.label: API Key
apiKey.help: Generate in n8n Settings -> API. Use keyRef to an env var, not inline.

enableEdit.label: Enable Edit Tools
enableEdit.help: Exposes all workflow + execution lifecycle write tools. Workflows: create, save (confirm-gated, auto-backup), activate, deactivate, archive (reversible soft-delete), unarchive, delete-workflow (confirm-gated, snapshot-before-delete), pin/unpin node data. Executions: cancel, retry, retry-executions (batch, confirm-gated, capped at 50 ids), delete, delete-executions (batch fan-out, confirm-gated, idempotent on 404, 5xx aborts). Tags: create, delete (cascade-warned, confirm-gated), set-workflow-tags (replaces, no confirm — reversible). Prefer archive over delete-workflow for cleanup that preserves the id. Credential writes (create-credential, delete-credential) require an additional opt-in via enableCredentialsWrite.

enableCredentialsWrite.label: Enable Credential Writes
enableCredentialsWrite.help: Second gate for the credential write tools (create-credential, delete-credential). Off by default. Required IN ADDITION to enableEdit — turning on enableEdit alone never exposes credential writes. Reason: create-credential is the only tool where agent input carries plaintext secrets, and delete-credential cascades to break every workflow that references the credential. Read tools (list-credentials, get-credential-schema, find-workflows-using-credential) are always available regardless of this flag.

maxExecutionLogBytes.label: Max Log Bytes
maxExecutionLogBytes.help: Truncate large execution logs to keep tool responses within token budgets.
